channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: As Romania prepares to phase out coal by 2032, some miners are retraining for jobs in renewable energies.
item.dc:creator: Anca Gurzu, Cipher News, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Floods in Philippines kill at least 7 and trap others on roofs as storm approaches
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Torrential rain set off by an approaching tropical storm swamped the eastern Philippines overnight with widespread flooding that killed at least seven people, trapped others on their roofs and sparked frantic appeals for help, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon chief: North Korean troops are in Russia
item.description: Secretary of Defense Lloyd Austin said North Korean troops appear to be in Russia. “We are seeing evidence that there are North Korean troops that have, that have gone to … Russia,” Austin told reporters in Rome on Wednesday. “What exactly they’re doing, left to be seen.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Charles gets a warm welcome on his first visit as king to Samoa. But uneasy questions await
item.description: Britain's King Charles III and his wife Queen Camilla have arrived in Samoa ahead of a biennial summit of 56 leaders of Commonwealth countries.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay And Ayaka Mcgill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Belarus sets a Jan. 26 election that's almost certain to extend its authoritarian leader's rule
item.description: Lawmakers in Belarus set the next presidential election for Jan. 26, a vote almost certain to extend the three-decade rule of authoritarian leader Alexander Lukashenko.
item.dc:creator: Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Indigenous Australian Senator Lidia Thorpe intensifies criticism of King Charles III
item.description: CANBERRA, Australia (AP) — An Indigenous senator has intensified her criticism of King Charles III, again accusing the British monarch of complicity in the “genocide” against Australia’s First Nations peoples and declaring on Wednesday she will not be “shut down.” Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US defense chief Austin says there’s evidence North Korea has sent troops to Russia
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin says there is evidence that North Korea has sent troops to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blinken urges Israel to seek enduring deal, as officials say a limited cease-fire has been discussed
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken says Israel needs to pursue an “enduring strategic success” in Gaza after its tactical victories against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Villagers are wary of plans to dam a river to ensure Panama Canal's water supply
item.description: EL JOBO, Panama (AP) — A long, wooden boat puttered down the Indio River’s chocolate waters carrying Ana María Antonio and a colleague from the Panama Canal Authority on a mission to hear directly from villagers who could be affected by plans to dam the river.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Desperation amid search for survivors of an airstrike on a crowded area near major Beirut hospital
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Nearly 16 hours after an Israeli airstrike hit across the street from Beirut’s main public hospital, rescuers were still removing debris Tuesday from the overcrowded slum area.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea warns it can send arms to Ukraine after reports of North's troops in Russia
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea warned Tuesday it could consider supplying weapons to Ukraine in response to North Korea allegedly dispatching troops to Russia, as both North Korea and Russia denied the movements. NATO's secretary general said that would mark a “significant escalation.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard officer charged in plot to kill American author in US
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment against four Iranian officials Tuesday for their alleged involvement in a murder-for-hire targeting Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Woman becomes stuck upside down between boulders for 7 hours while trying to retrieve dropped phone
item.description: A woman who became trapped in between two boulders in Australia after falling into a crevice while trying to retrieve her phone has been saved, NSW Ambulance says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European Union observers flag irregularities and 'alteration of results' in Mozambique election
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — European Union election observers have flagged irregularities in Mozambique's election as the country awaits the announcement of official results this week amid protests by opposition supporters and the recent killing of two prominent opposition figures.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 transgender women stabbed to death at home in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two men armed with daggers killed two transgender women at their home in conservative northwest Pakistan overnight before fleeing the scene, police said, a sign of increasing violence against trans people in the country.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says only a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway a reluctant Germany
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his so-called “victory plan” after the U.S. election, but concedes that its key demand — an invitation to join NATO — is not welcome by some major Western partners, in particular Germany.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mystery of downed airplane in Sudan deepens as Kyrgyzstan insists aircraft had been de-registered
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — The mystery surrounding a crashed cargo plane in Sudan purportedly downed by the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces deepened Tuesday as authorities insisted the aircraft had been de-registered in Kyrgyzstan.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar swirls toward the Bahamas after hitting Cuba as a hurricane
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Oscar chugged toward the Bahamas on Tuesday after making landfall in Cuba as a Category 1 hurricane, killing at least six people as it unleashed heavy rains on an island also hit by an unrelated massive power outage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing student in Spain was likely crushed, incinerated after passing out in trash while partying: cops
item.description: A student missing in Majorca, Spain, was likely crushed to death in a garbage truck before she was incinerated at a waste disposal plant, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gunmen kill 7 people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Gunmen fatally shot at least seven people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir and injured at least five others, officials said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken heads to Middle East in wake of Sinwar's killing
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is going to the Middle East in the wake of the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Voting from abroad: What to know
item.description: Most U.S. citizens who reside outside the country can vote absentee in primary and general elections.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea demands withdrawal of North Korean troops allegedly helping Russia fight Ukraine
item.description: South Korea issued a demand to Russia to condemn the cooperation between Russia and North Korea, calling for the withdrawal of North Korean troops from Russia's war with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American reportedly kidnapped in Philippine coastal town; police investigating
item.description: Police in the Philippines are searching for American national Elliot Onil Eastman, from Vermont, after receiving reports gunmen had kidnapped him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF releases video showing Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar fleeing into tunnel with family before Oct. 7 attack
item.description: The IDF released footage showing Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar fleeing underground into a tunnel in Gaza in the hours leading up to the Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NHC monitoring Hurricane Oscar as it nears Cuba
item.description: Hurricane Oscar is being closely monitored by the National Hurricane Center as it makes its way toward Cuba on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Japan's governing party headquarters is attacked with firebombs and suspect is arrested
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s governing party in Tokyo on Saturday, then crashed his car into the fencing of the prime minister's residence, police said. There were no reports of injuries.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Strike on Netanyahu home 'a signal' Iran proxies not incapacitated, expert says
item.description: A recent drone strike targeting the home of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending "a signal" that Iran-backed militant groups are not "incapacitated," despite losing senior leadership in recent weeks, according to a Middle East expert. Daniel Levy, the president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea troops readying for Ukraine combat: South Korea
item.description: South Korea said it's used facial recognition AI tech to identify North Korean officers in eastern Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ju-min Park, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah vows a new phase in the war, as Israel says it has killed Hamas leader Sinwar
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Lebanese militant group Hezbollah vowed to launch a new phase of fighting against invading Israeli troops Friday, a day after Israel said its forces in the Palestinian enclave of Gaza had killed Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, the chief architect of last year’s brutal attack on Israe
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel pressured by UN and US to step up action to tackle Gaza's escalating humanitarian crisis
item.description: Israel’s U.N. Ambassador Danny Danon insisted that his country’s humanitarian efforts remain “as comprehensive as ever.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation
